source.title: A spatial threshold for astrocyte calcium surge

confidence_rationale: In vivo two-photon imaging in mice identifies a precise spatial threshold (>23% of astrocyte arborization activated) that determines whether Ca activity remains localized to domains or spreads cell-wide to trigger gliotransmitter release. This is the most directly quantitative answer in the literature to how spatial extent of activation governs R-field output. High confidence because the finding is empirical (not purely modeling), has mechanistic grounding (IP3R2), and the spatial threshold concept maps directly to R(x,t) resolution design.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Sensory-evoked astrocyte Ca responses originate and remain localized in domains of the astrocytic arborization but propagate to the entire cell if a spatial threshold of >23% of the arborization being activated is surpassed. IP3R2 is required for calcium surge. The spatial threshold of the Ca signal determines gliotransmitter release.
mapping.ree_translation: R(x,t) in REE has two regimes: a locally-confined regime where activity below a spatial threshold produces domain-specific modulation of nearby synapses only, and a global-spread regime above threshold where R exerts cell-wide (or territory-wide) regulatory influence and triggers gliotransmitter-equivalent output. For REE's residue field, this means the appropriate spatial resolution is not fixed -- it is state-dependent, with a threshold nonlinearity. Below threshold, R should be resolved at domain (sub-territory) scale. Above threshold, R spreads to territory scale.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The 23% threshold is measured relative to a single astrocyte's arborization area in mouse cortex under sensory stimulation. The corresponding threshold in REE latent space (what fraction of concurrently active feature representations triggers a global R update) requires separate calibration. This paper establishes the principle and rough magnitude but the exact threshold is species- and context-specific.
mapping.source_context: Mice, in vivo two-photon imaging of cortical astrocytes during sensory stimulation; IP3R2 knockout mice for mechanistic validation; in situ electrophysiological recordings for gliotransmitter output.

confidence_components.notes: Published in eLife, in vivo two-photon imaging with genetic validation (IP3R2 KO) and electrophysiological output confirmation. The spatial threshold finding is quantitatively precise and directly relevant to Q-002. Mapping fidelity is the highest of the three Q-002 entries because the threshold nonlinearity concept maps cleanly to R(x,t) regime design. Transfer risk is low conceptually but the specific 23% value should not be imported numerically into REE without recalibration.
